pending.body: src/adapters/process_backend.rs:61-86; src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:221-225,2587-2593: `is_transient_codex_failure` applies its substring heuristics to any `AppError::InvocationFailed`, regardless of the existing `failure_class`. That means a terminal `DomainValidationFailure` whose validation message happens to mention `503`, `timed out`, `rate limit`, etc. will be retried as transient, and an `InvocationFailed { failure_class: BackendExhausted, details: "Rate limit reached, try again at ..." }` will also burn the transient retry budget before the panel degrades. Both cases violate the bead contract: domain-validation failures must not be retried, and exhausted-backend handling is supposed to stay unchanged.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 11 -> Plan and Implement
